Tests that `String.prototype.charCodeAt` returns NaN for an out-of-bounds index.

# Purpose
This code is a single assertion statement that tests the behavior of the `String.prototype.charCodeAt` method in JavaScript. It checks if calling `charCodeAt` with an invalid index of `-1` on the string `"abc"` returns `NaN`. The `assert` function is used to verify that the result of `String("abc").charCodeAt(-1)` is indeed `NaN`, which is the expected behavior when the index is out of the valid range for the string. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific edge case for the `charCodeAt` method.
